title: Japanese footballer Kaoru Mitoma runs over a female cyclist in alleged red-light violation
summary: The Japanese national team player collided with a bicycle at an intersection, leaving the woman riding it injured.

title: Tensions rise between the United States and Iran with mutual attacks and Trump's threats
summary: The US president declares the ceasefire over while Iran bombs military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, raising the price of crude oil.

title: Japan's Diet begins debate on reform of the Imperial House Law to ensure stable succession
summary: After weeks of deadlock, government and opposition agree to start discussion in exchange for the prime minister's appearance.

title: NATO agrees on a 13 trillion yen aid package for Ukraine and reinforces military support
summary: At the Alliance summit, leaders adopt an assistance plan while Japan seeks to align its stance in the absence of the prime minister.

title: Corruption scandal shakes Fukuoka assembly over demand for money in exchange for political favors
summary: The president of the regional parliament orders an investigation into all members after a complaint that a lawmaker received one million yen.

title: Regional bank Zenshin goes bankrupt with a debt of 115.1 billion yen and signs of fraud appear
summary: The bankruptcy affects thousands of retailers and the Japanese government offers interest-free loans to mitigate the impact.

title: Japan expands computer science and technology education in elementary and junior high schools with new subjects
summary: The Ministry of Education plans to double teaching hours in technology and bring forward its implementation starting from the 2028 school year.

title: Toyama Airport rebranded as "Toyama-Takayama Sushi Airport" to attract foreign tourists
summary: The name change, which includes a town from another prefecture, sparks debate about its effectiveness in boosting tourism.

title: Four years since the assassination of former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe, tributes follow
summary: Relatives, politicians, and citizens remember the former leader in an event where they also ask not to forget the issue of the Unification Church.

title: Two former employees of an insurance company arrested for defrauding 1.25 million yen in medical benefits
summary: The women submitted false insurance applications to collect compensation they were not entitled to.

title: An electric power company loses SSDs with customer data and announces internal disciplinary measures
summary: The lost media contained contractual information and the company bans the use of unauthorized external devices.

title: A chief prosecutor sanctioned for maintaining an improper relationship with a suspect in a hidden funds case
summary: The prosecutor, who was leading the investigation into the opaque funds case, was removed from his post and subjected to disciplinary proceedings.

title: IMF cuts global growth forecast for 2026 to 3.0% due to higher energy costs
summary: The international organization warns that inflation and geopolitical uncertainty will weigh on global economic activity.

title: Trump threatens to suspend trade with Spain, calling it a "hostile country" to the United States
summary: The US president states that Spain is an enemy of the US and will take unprecedented trade retaliation.

title: Japan strengthens preparedness for high-risk infectious diseases such as Ebola
summary: The Ministry of Health will designate reference hospitals and update protocols to treat serious patients.

title: Floods in southern China release nearly 900 snakes from a farm and cause the death of a woman
summary: Torrential rains destroyed a breeding facility, causing a massive escape of snakes that left one fatality.

title: Nordic combined excluded from the program of the 2030 Winter Olympic Games
summary: The discipline, in which Japan was a power, will not be part of the next Olympic event, causing sadness among athletes.

title: Woman arrested in Japan for sewing her roommate's lips shut with surgical thread
summary: The attacker, who allegedly acted with an accomplice, was arrested after the victim's complaint, who suffered serious injuries.

title: Former police chief Yukihiko Inoue, who led the response to the sarin gas attack on the Tokyo subway, dies at 88
summary: The police authority who commanded the investigations into the Aum Shinrikyo attack and the murder of the police chief has died.

title: UN warns that only 15% of the Sustainable Development Goals are on track to be achieved
summary: The UN report indicates that armed conflicts and poverty have reversed years of global progress.
